projectDescription: A 16-week guided program helping users transition off GLP-1 medications through personalized hunger coaching, CGM-based glucose insights, and progressive lifestyle habit building.

screens.name: Onboarding — Welcome
screens.purpose: Program overview and start button

screens.name: Onboarding — Name & Age
screens.purpose: Collect user name and age

screens.name: Onboarding — Medication History
screens.purpose: Medication type, duration, and current status

screens.name: Onboarding — Why Coming Off GLP-1
screens.purpose: Multi-select reasons for discontinuing medication

screens.name: Onboarding — Body & Activity
screens.purpose: Weight, goal weight, height, activity level

screens.name: Onboarding — Goals
screens.purpose: Multi-select goals for the program

screens.name: Onboarding — Pre-GLP-1 Struggles
screens.purpose: Multi-select struggles before medication

screens.name: Onboarding — Current Struggles
screens.purpose: Ranked multi-select struggles that shape block order

screens.name: Onboarding — Strength Training
screens.purpose: Strength training experience level (6 options)

screens.name: Onboarding — Assessment Question 1
screens.purpose: Baseline self-assessment quiz (rated 1-5)

screens.name: Onboarding — Assessment Question 5
screens.purpose: Mid-point of baseline assessment quiz

screens.name: Onboarding — Your Personalized Program
screens.purpose: Shows personalized 16-week roadmap and per-question feedback

screens.name: Home — CGM Active
screens.purpose: Dashboard with live CGM graph, graduation card, hunger coach, GPA strip

screens.name: Home — CGM Off
screens.purpose: During off-sensor blocks, the dashboard shifts from live glucose to an average-day glucose graph from the last sensor period. The Hunger Coach is elevated as the user's primary biofeedback tool, teaching them to read internal hunger signals without relying on CGM data — the core skill needed for life after graduation.
annotations: Hunger Coach elevated as PRIMARY SIGNAL THIS WEEK
Shows avg glucose, GPA, time-in-range from last sensor

screens.name: Graduation Toolkit
screens.purpose: Education hub with scrollable timeline, core principles, block goal, education sections

screens.name: Block 1 — Baseline
screens.purpose: Block 1 goal: Observe food responses, activity, sleep, hunger with CGM

screens.name: Block 2 — Nutrition
screens.purpose: Nutrition-focused block: protein and fiber goals

screens.name: Block 3 — Movement
screens.purpose: Movement block: walking, steps, resistance training with CGM

screens.name: Block 4 — Full System
screens.purpose: Satiating meals, sleep, and stress with all habits maintained

screens.name: Block 5 — Locked
screens.purpose: Locked state showing check-in prompt for Blocks 5-8

flows.name: First-Time User Onboarding
flows.description: New user completes 18-step onboarding: profile, health history, goals, baseline self-assessment quiz, and personalized 16-week roadmap generation.

steps.action: Multi-selects reasons for coming off GLP-1
Enters weight, goal weight, height, activity level
Reviews personalized 16-week roadmap and per-question feedback, taps Start

flows.name: Daily Hunger Check-In
flows.description: User logs daily hunger/fullness (1-5) from the Log tab or Home screen. Gets glucose-aware insight card. High hunger or extreme fullness triggers coach escalation.

steps.annotation: Also accessible from Home hunger CTA

steps.action: Sees glucose insight card, micro-tip, updated satiety skill score
steps.annotation: Coach escalation button if hunger >= 4 or fullness extreme

steps.action: Taps 'Need help? Talk to your coach'
steps.annotation: Triggered when hunger >= 4 or fullness <= 1 or >= 5 | Subflow: Coach Escalation (High Hunger)

steps.action: Taps 'Need help? Talk to your coach'
steps.annotation: Triggered when hunger >= 4 or fullness <= 1 or >= 5

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Still Hungry After Eating
flows.description: User ate but is still hungry. Coach assesses timing, drills into meal composition, and differentiates physical vs hedonic hunger through branching questions.

steps.action: Sees timing question: When did you eat?

steps.annotation: Vagus nerve delay + 10-min timer | Subflow: Under 15 Minutes — Wait for Signals

steps.action: Taps '15-30 min' → What did you eat?

steps.annotation: Spike-crash cycle explanation | Subflow: 15-30 Min → Carb-Heavy Meal
Subflow: 15-30 Min → Had Protein → Physical Hunger
Trust the signal | Subflow: 15-30 Min → Had Protein → Physical Hunger
Flavor reset | Subflow: 15-30 Min → Had Protein → Mouth Hunger
May need larger portions | Subflow: 15-30 Min → Balanced Meal

steps.action: Taps '15-30 min' → What did you eat?

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Starving
flows.description: User is starving. Coach assesses last meal timing and provides urgency-appropriate guidance.

steps.annotation: Eat now, protein first, set alarm for tomorrow | Subflow: 4+ Hours Ago — Emergency Refuel
Protein bridge snack + CGM check | Subflow: 2-4 Hours Ago — Meal Composition Issue
Metabolic recovery — positive sign | Subflow: Just Woke Up — Morning Recovery
Eat now, protein first, set alarm for tomorrow

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Craving
flows.description: User has a craving. Coach identifies the type (sweet/salty/rich/specific) and provides biochemically-informed strategies.

steps.annotation: Berries + yogurt, dark chocolate alternatives | Subflow: Sweet — Glucose Dip
Water first, crunchy alternatives | Subflow: Salty — Dehydration/Stress
Healthy fat integration | Subflow: Rich/Fatty — Fat Deficiency
Timer + flexible restraint | Subflow: Specific Food — 10-Minute Rule

flows.name: Hunger Coach — HALT Check
flows.description: User isn't hungry but wants to eat. HALT grid identifies emotional driver: Hungry, Angry/Anxious, Lonely/Bored, or Tired. Each leads to targeted non-food coping strategies.

steps.action: Selects 'Not hungry but want to eat'

steps.annotation: Low-level hunger hard to detect post-GLP-1 | Subflow: H — Actually Hungry
Box breathing 4-4-4-4 + 10-min timer | Subflow: A — Angry/Anxious/Stressed
Social connection, environment change | Subflow: L — Lonely/Bored
20-min nap > eating, sleep debt cycle | Subflow: T — Tired/Exhausted
Low-level hunger hard to detect post-GLP-1

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Body Scan (Unsure)
flows.description: User isn't sure if hungry. Coach guides 4-step timed body scan for interoception. After scan, user identifies their state from 4 options, each with targeted advice.

steps.annotation: Eat with protein | Subflow: Physical Signals — Real Hunger
Body needs fuel | Subflow: Boring Food Test — Likely Hungry
10-min wait, craving peaks at 5-7 min | Subflow: Only Indulgent — Craving
15-min timer + water | Subflow: Still Unsure — Wait and Check

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Unusual Time Hunger
flows.description: User is hungry at an unusual time. Coach identifies the timing pattern and provides circadian/physiological explanations.

steps.annotation: Kitchen closes at 8pm rule | Subflow: Late Night — Habit/Fatigue
Preventive protein snack at 2:30pm | Subflow: Afternoon — Circadian Dip
Positive sign, protein-heavy breakfast | Subflow: Early Morning — Metabolic Recovery
30-60 min anabolic window | Subflow: Post-Workout — Legitimate Refuel

flows.name: Hunger Coach — About to Eat
flows.description: User is about to eat and checks in. Coach assesses hunger level and provides level-specific meal pacing advice with a 20-minute timer.

steps.annotation: Protein-dense smaller meal + 20-min timer | Subflow: Level 1-2 — Not Really Hungry
Protein first, stop at fullness 3-4 + 20-min timer | Subflow: Level 3 — Perfect Hunger
Water first, protein first, fork-down + 20-min timer | Subflow: Level 4-5 — Very Hungry
Protein first, stop at fullness 3-4 + 20-min timer
Water first, protein first, fork-down + 20-min timer

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Post-Meal Reflection
flows.description: After eating, user reflects on fullness. Direct outcomes (just right, too full, can't tell) get targeted advice. 'Still hungry' branches into a deeper meal composition drill-down.

steps.action: How do you feel after eating?

steps.annotation: Note the meal, template concept | Subflow: Just Right — Satisfied
Data not failure, don't skip next meal | Subflow: Too Full — Overshoot
15-min timer, vagus nerve delay | Subflow: Can't Tell — Wait for Signals

steps.action: Taps 'Still hungry' → What did you eat?

steps.annotation: Protein anchor strategy | Subflow: Still Hungry → Low Protein
Increase without guilt | Subflow: Still Hungry → Small Portion
15-min wait + slower next meal | Subflow: Still Hungry → Ate Too Fast
Body genuinely needs more | Subflow: Still Hungry → Balanced Meal
Data not failure, don't skip next meal

steps.action: Taps 'Still hungry' → What did you eat?

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Hunger Changing
flows.description: User's hunger patterns are shifting during GLP-1 transition. Coach identifies the type of change and provides reassurance with timelines for normalization.

steps.annotation: 4-8 week normalization, not losing control | Subflow: More Hungry — Ghrelin Rebound
Structured meals, protein shakes | Subflow: Less Hungry — Protect Muscle
Consistent schedule anchors rhythm | Subflow: Unpredictable — Waves
5 types of hunger framework | Subflow: Different Sensation — Hunger Literacy

flows.name: Hunger Coach — Teach Me About Hunger
flows.description: User wants to learn about hunger science. Coach presents 5 mini-lessons: types of hunger, hunger scale, GLP-1 effects, satiation, glucose-hunger.

flows.name: Coach Follow-Up Outcome
flows.description: After any coach response, user reports what happened. Coach provides positive reinforcement regardless of outcome — building a feedback loop where every action is progress.

steps.action: Any coach response screen shows follow-up buttons
steps.annotation: Representative example — applies to all coach responses

flows.name: Self-Assessment Check-In
flows.description: Periodic check-ins at 3 program checkpoints: Block 1 baseline, Block 3 mid-program, and Block 5 post-foundation. Each uses the same 4-step flow (insights → quiz → feedback → focus selection) with stage-customized copy. The Block 5 version unlocks Blocks 5-8.

steps.action: Rates 8 questions 1-5 (hunger, nutrition, exercise, sleep, glucose, stress, fullness, weight)
steps.annotation: Same quiz format at all 3 checkpoints

steps.action: Reviews per-question feedback with progress bars and area rankings
steps.annotation: Color-coded red/yellow/green, delta from baseline at later checkpoints

steps.action: Reviews starting numbers and Signos-noticed insights
steps.annotation: Establishes baseline scores — completed during onboarding | Subflow: Baseline Assessment (Block 1)

steps.action: Reviews progress numbers compared to Block 1
steps.annotation: Delta indicators vs baseline | Subflow: Mid-Program Assessment (Block 3)

steps.action: Reviews full progress numbers and Signos insights
Selects 1-2 focus areas to personalize Blocks 5-8
steps.annotation: Unlocks weeks 9-16 | Subflow: Post-Foundation Assessment (Block 5) — Unlock Blocks 5-8

steps.action: Reviews starting numbers and Signos-noticed insights
steps.annotation: Establishes baseline scores — completed during onboarding

steps.action: Reviews progress numbers compared to Block 1
Reviews full progress numbers and Signos insights
Reviews comprehensive feedback with full progress comparison
Selects 1-2 focus areas to personalize Blocks 5-8

flows.name: CGM Toggle
flows.description: User toggles between CGM-active (blue, live graph) and CGM-off (amber, average-day graph) dashboard states. The off-CGM view exists because 4 of 8 blocks have no sensor — during those blocks the Hunger Coach becomes the primary biofeedback tool, teaching users to read internal signals instead of relying on real-time glucose data. This builds the independence needed for life after graduation.

steps.annotation: Amber border, elevated hunger coach, average-day graph

flows.name: Explore Insights
flows.description: User navigates the three Insights tabs to review GPAs, sensor comparisons, hunger correlations, and graduation trends. Deferred to Phase 2 to accelerate initial launch — existing GPA strip on Home provides sufficient score visibility for Phase 1.

steps.action: Views Lifestyle GPA, Transition Readiness, hunger correlations
Taps Glucose tab — views Glucose GPA, sensor-by-sensor comparison, glucose correlations
Taps Graduation tab — views trend graph, score deltas, program progress

flows.name: Log & Body Scanner
flows.description: User accesses the central logging hub and launches the camera-based body composition scan.

steps.action: Views logging hub (hunger, food, exercise, water, sleep, glucose, body scanner)
Taps Body Scanner, starts scan, views results (body fat %, visceral fat %, lifestyle bonus)

flows.name: Weekly Check-In
flows.description: Each week the app auto-prompts a check-in modal on the home screen: mood (5-emoji), confidence (1-5), biggest challenge, and education progress.

steps.action: Rates mood and confidence, enters challenge text, reviews education progress

flows.name: Settings & Program Management
flows.description: User views profile and program details, or resets the program to start fresh.

steps.action: Views user profile, program link, maintenance band, preferences
Reviews detailed metrics, dose history, graduation readiness score

steps.annotation: Returns to step 0 | Subflow: Reset Program

steps.action: Reviews detailed metrics, dose history, graduation readiness score
Taps Reset program, confirms in modal

flows.name: Graduation Toolkit Walkthrough
flows.description: User explores the 16-week program through the Graduation Toolkit, browsing each block's goals, education modules, and interactive games. Each block detail shows the block's focus, CGM status, and assigned education.

steps.action: Views 16-week timeline, core principles, education sections
Taps Block 1 — reviews baseline + CGM goal
Taps Block 2 — reviews protein + fiber goals
Taps Block 3 — reviews walking + resistance goals
Taps Block 4 — reviews satiating meals + sleep/stress
Taps Block 5 — sees locked state with check-in prompt
